leadText: The Spear Pillar (Japanese: 槍の柱 Spear Pillar) is a location on the peak of Mount Coronet in Sinnoh where the Legendary Pokémon Dialga, Palkia, and Giratina are known to enter the region from their respective dimensions and from which, legends say, Sinnoh itself came from. It is the present-day ruins of the Temple of Sinnoh. As the Spear Pillar is part of Mount Coronet, it has a special magnetic field that causes Magneton and Nosepass to evolve into Magnezone and Probopass, respectively, when leveled up here.

sections.title: In the core series games
sections.text: Contents 1 In the core series games 1.1 History 1.1.1 Pokémon Legends: Arceus 1.1.2 Pokémon Diamond, Pearl, Brilliant Diamond, and Shining Pearl 1.1.3 Pokémon Platinum 1.1.4 Post-game 1.2 Items 1.3 Special encounters 1.3.1 Pokémon Diamond and Pearl 1.3.2 Pokémon Platinum 1.3.3 Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl 1.4 Trainers 1.4.1 Pokémon Diamond and Pearl 1.4.2 Pokémon Platinum 1.4.3 Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl 1.5 Gallery 1.5.1 Artwork 1.5.2 Screenshots 1.6 Layout 1.7 Music In the core series games [ edit | edit source ] History [ edit | edit source ] Spoiler warning: this article may contain major plot or ending details. Dialga being controlled by the Red Chain Pokémon Legends: Arceus [ edit | edit source ] The Temple of Sinnoh took on its current, ruined appearance when Dialga / Palkia changed into its Origin Forme to battle the player , destroying most of it in the process. The Survey Corps of the Galaxy Team coined the name "Spear Pillar" to refer to the now-ruined temple. Pokémon Diamond, Pearl , Brilliant Diamond, and Shining Pearl [ edit | edit source ] Cyrus uses a Red Chain created from gems extracted from the bodies of the lake guardians , Uxie , Mesprit , and Azelf to summon Dialga D BD / Palkia P SP and have it start create a new universe for himself, free of emotion. The process creates distortions in time D BD /dimensions P SP , which are visible throughout Sinnoh . Mars and Jupiter battle against the player and their rival Barry , standing in the way before the player can reach Cyrus. However, before his new world can be born, the lake guardians arrive after being freed by the player and use their powers to destroy the Red Chain, freeing the Legendary Pokémon from Cyrus's control. Angered by this, Cyrus then battles the player and after he's defeated, he and the other Team Galactic members leave Spear Pillar, as Cyrus swears to one day succeed in his plans. Afterwards, Professor Rowan shows up with their assistent Dawn / Lucas and the player has a chance to battle and catch the Legendary Pokémon. Pokémon Platinum [ edit | edit source ] Giratina appears before Cyrus In this game, Cyrus summons both Dialga and Palkia to Spear Pillar through the use of two Red Chains : one created from the gems extracted from the lake guardians, and the other being artificially created as a copy of the former. The power of Dialga and Palkia together is too great for the lake trio to counterbalance as they did in the previous games, and so the Pokémon world is shortly doomed. Just when Cyrus believes himself to have won, he feels the presence of Giratina , angered by his actions to destroy the world. Giratina appears between Cyrus and the other two, warping the ancient pillars. It grabs Cyrus, warping him into the Distortion World and leaving behind the tunnel between the worlds. Dialga and Palkia vanish back into their own dimensions , and Cynthia requests the player's help in the Distortion World to repair the link between the two worlds and prevent the warping of reality caused by Giratina's entrance from extending throughout. After Giratina and Cyrus are defeated in the Distortion World, the player ends up in the Pokémon world once more at the entrance to Turnback Cave . Once the player has entered the Hall of Fame and found both the Adamant and Lustrous Orbs in Mount Coronet, Cynthia's grandmother will tell the player that in ancient times, people apparently met with Dialga and Palkia, by having the Adamant and Lustrous Orbs. Returning to the Spear Pillar with these items, the player will be able to battle with and potentially catch Dialga and Palkia. Post-game [ edit | edit source ] If the player has the Azure Flute upon returning to the Spear Pillar after obtaining the National Pokédex , blowing into it will cause a staircase leading to the Hall of Origin to appear. After reaching the top of the staircase, the player will automatically approach the Mythical Pokémon Arceus. In Generation IV , this event is not legitimately accessible, but is available in Generation VIII if the console has completed save data from Pokémon Legends: Arceus . Spoilers end here. Items [ edit | edit source ] Item Location Games Adamant Orb North of Dialga D BD Lustrous Orb North of Palkia P SP Special encounters [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Diamond and Pearl [ edit | edit source ] A Dialga D / Palkia P appears here as part of the story. If run from, it respawns upon leaving the area.
